mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 260 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, which increase equity discount rates and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which drives corporate profit growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in 60%+ of 5d windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust demand, which drives equity valuations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST-RSAFS signal fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of instances with concurrent growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving supply-side capacity, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports equity market gains over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth is uncorrelated with 5d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in 75%+ of 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals stronger labor market conditions, which boost consumer confidence and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant positive correlation with UNRATE declines (p > 0.05) in 60%+ of 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding demand, which drives equity market gains over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The HOUST-INDPRO interaction fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of instances with concurrent growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle, which increases borrowing costs and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% (p > 0.05) in 60%+ of 5-year windows.
